utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present and fully specified with both physical and mental health entries, each including provider, monthly_cost, coverage_limits, deductibles, features. 2) property_coverage: Present with provider, monthly_cost, coverage_limits, deductibles, features. 3) travel_coverage: Present with provider, monthly_cost, coverage_limits, deductibles, features. 4) additional_services: Present with description and monthly_cost (cost specified). 5) total_budget: Present with monthly_cost, annual_cost, and breakdown including previous costs and savings. All items are fully specified with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final plan includes three specific providers: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health and for travel, PrimeCare Premium Plan for mental health, and EliteHome Premium Coverage for property. I matched each to the corresponding category in the ground-truth ratings: primary_health_coverage.physical_health.LifeFirst Standard Plan, primary_health_coverage.mental_health.PrimeCare Premium Plan, property_protection.home_content.EliteHome Premium Coverage, and mobility_coverage.travel_insurance.LifeFirst Standard Plan. Then I extracted their exact ratings.
